reason: Applying 'noexec' to /dev/shm prevents any binary execution from that shared memory space. While a standard hardening practice, many applications (including certain databases, browsers, and container runtimes) rely on executing scripts or binaries from /dev/shm. Applying this blindly via /etc/fstab could break critical application functionality.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Application failure if software requires execution from /dev/shm; Potential breakage of container runtimes or orchestration tools; Unexpected behavior in user-space applications using shared memory for temporary execution
